title: branching-proof-scrubber widget params
description: Tree-shaped sibling of `proof-scrubber`. Walks a proof whose middle is a fork — the reader picks a tactic (proof by contradiction vs. by construction, case A vs. case B vs. neither, induction vs. direct, etc.) and the consequent chain plays out. Renders the full proof as a top-down tree (one rounded rect per node) with the chosen path highlighted in the configured palette accent, dead branches greyed out with a red ✗, and untraversed branches dimmed. Below the tree sits a card showing the current node's prompt (KaTeX-rendered), prev/next step buttons, and at fork nodes a row of branch buttons labeled with each child's `branchLabel`. A breadcrumb traces the chosen path; a reset button returns to the root. Self-contained IIFE in renderScript — no page-global library dependency. Both exports are pure functions of params.

widgetId.description: DOM id for the outer <div class="widget"> wrapper. All inner element ids are derived from this so multiple instances coexist on a page.

title.description: Display title rendered inside .hd > .ttl. May contain $…$ LaTeX (rendered by the page's KaTeX auto-render pass).

hint.description: Optional short hint rendered inside .hd > .hint. Defaults to a generic 'click a node or pick a branch · use ←/→' string when omitted.

rootId.description: ID of the root node (the proof's starting point). Must match the `id` of an entry in `nodes`.

nodes.description: Flat array of step objects keyed by `id`. Each node has a `kind` (step / fork / leaf), a `prompt` shown when the reader scrubs to it, and a `children` array referencing other node ids. Sequential `step` nodes have exactly one child; `fork` nodes have ≥2 children; `leaf` nodes have zero. The widget walks the implied tree via `rootId` + `children`.

id.description: Kebab-case id, unique within this widget instance.

kind.description: `step` = single-child sequential node. `fork` = multi-child decision point; the reader picks a child via labeled buttons. `leaf` = terminal node (QED, contradiction, or end of a case).

prompt.description: HTML body rendered in the readout when this node is the current step. Supports KaTeX delimiters ($…$, $$…$$, \(…\), \[…\]) — the widget re-typesets the readout after each step change.

children.description: Ordered list of child node ids. `step` requires length 1, `fork` requires length ≥2, `leaf` requires length 0. All ids must reference entries in `nodes`.

branchLabel.description: Short label (5-25 chars) shown on the branch picker button when this node is reached via a fork. Examples: 'by contradiction', 'case A: x ∈ S', 'try induction'. Required only on children of fork nodes; ignored elsewhere.

dead.description: If true, this node (and the subtree rooted at it) is a teaching dead-end — a branch that leads to a contradiction or fails to prove the claim. Renders with a red ✗ glyph and a muted color even when on the chosen path.

width.description: SVG viewport width in user units. The CSS layer caps display at 100% of the container so this also controls layout density.

height.description: SVG viewport height in user units. Increase when the tree is deep (>5 levels).

palette.description: Project accent color used to highlight the chosen path through the tree. Resolved via `var(--<palette>)` at render time so the page theme governs the actual hue.

sectionComment.description: ARTIFACT. Optional banner comment preserved when migrating sources, emitted as `/* <sectionComment> */` between the leading <script> tag and the IIFE opener.
